rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE ROSE G. PAVAO LIVING TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 27, 2008
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Janice Pavao Belen, Individually And As Trustee Of The Rose G. Pavao Living Trust'S Notice Of Demurrer And Motion To Strike Petition To Set Aside Trust
rulings.Rulings: Demurrer by Janice Pavao Belen individually and as Trustee of the Rose G. Pavao Living Trust Dated September 27, 2008 Demurrer: 2nd count for Tortious Interference with Right to Inherit - Sustain without leave to amend. CCP 430.10(e) 3rd count - Breach of Trust and Fiduciary Duty (as to 2008 Trust) - Sustain with 10 days leave to amend. CCP 430.10(e). No facts alleged regarding breach of 2008 trust. 4th count - Constructive Trust (as to 2008 Trust) - This is a remedy, not a cause of action. Sustain with 10 days leave to amend. Leave to amend is sustained for purposes of including this remedy in the prayer for relief. CCP 430.10(e). 5th count - Resulting Trust -. This is a remedy, not a cause of action. Sustain with 10 days leave to amend. Leave to amend is sustained for purposes of including this remedy in the prayer for relief, assuming that there are sufficient facts stated in any of the causes of action which could support this remedy. CCP 430.10(e). 6th count - Rescission and/or Restitution - This is a remedy, not a cause of action. Sustain with 10 days leave to amend. Leave to amend is sustained for purposes of including this remedy in the prayer for relief. Motion to strike: 1. 2nd count for Tortious Interference With Right to Inherit - off calendar 2. all claims for punitive damages contained in paragraph 47, page 8: lines 2-3 - denied 3. the 4th count for constructive trust in its entirety as it is improperly pled as a c/a - off calendar 4. the 5th count for resulting trust in its entirety as it is improperly pled as a c/a - off calendar 5. the 6th count for rescission and/or restitution in its entirety as it is improperly pled as a c/a - off calendar 6. page 9, line 27 for general damages - denied 7. page 10, line 5 (should be line 1) for special damages as to the 2nd and 3rd counts, as special damages arise from those counts for tortious interference and breach of trust and fiduciary duty which are properly the subject of Respondent
